title: Destructure-and-rebind in function head to avoid re-access
prompt:
Given:

```
def promote(user) do
  new_role = upgrade_role(user.role)
  %{user | role: new_role}
end
```

Refactor to use the `= <pattern>` bind-and-match form so you can destructure `role` from the struct in the head AND keep the whole-struct binding: `def promote(%User{role: role} = user) do new_role = upgrade_role(role); %{user | role: new_role} end`. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.UserPromotion do ... end` (with `alias MyApp.User`).
tier_rationale: The `%Struct{field: field} = var` bind-and-match form is non-obvious. Sonnet often destructures without re-binding or re-accesses via dot notation.
